transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good evening, team. Friday night build session. Let me frame where we are. It's been a productive day — Joshua confirmed newsletter and crash reporting are done, analytics scaffolding is the open item. Apple review is still pending. We have 12 uncommitted hub files, the analytics PR needs staging, and we're sitting at 7 faith lessons with a target of 15-20. The question tonight is: what do we ship in the next 2-3 hours that moves the needle hardest? Let's go around. Elon, you're carrying the heaviest load — status? Elon (CTO): Three things. First, the analytics Swift hooks PR — James and I got the green light from Joshua to stage it so he just reviews and merges. That's my top priority tonight, getting A-087 across the finish line. Second, the batch commit for the day-one patch — welcome modal copy, Compass empty state, podcast rendering fix — that's A-074 and it's 90% staged, I just need Steve's final Compass copy which I believe is done. Third, we've got 12 uncommitted hub files now, up from 7 this morning. I want to burn those down to zero tonight. The 3 dead-code TODO removals from A-076 are trivial, I'll knock those out between the bigger items.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: The analytics validation test environment is prepped and waiting — A-084 is ready to execute the moment Elon's hooks land. I've got pass/fail checks defined for all 6 event types: tab switches, lesson opened, lesson completed, time-on-lesson, session duration, and attribution. I also extended the implementation guide with crash breadcrumbs per A-059. Tonight I need Elon's PR to land so I can run validation. That's my only blocker.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Compass empty state copy was delivered hours ago via A-062. Welcome modal copy is done via A-067. Both are in Elon's hands. My focus tonight shifts to the 2 user-facing TODO items — A-050. Those empty states in Family and Compass need to feel intentional, not broken. I want to make sure the copy Elon wires in actually renders with the right hierarchy and spacing. I'll do a visual review once he's got the build compiling.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Revenue side — still zero active streams, which is expected pre-launch. Fear and Greed at 38, cash at $75K, both on autopilot per standing directives. The real action item for me tonight is closing out the launch metrics dashboard spec, A-054. I need that 150-download target framework locked with the day-3 escalation trigger so we're not scrambling when Apple approves. Also, the CMS pre-schedule flow test from A-081 — I want to do one final dry run tonight to make sure the one-click send is bulletproof. Monday follow-up newsletter coordination with Alex per A-091 is queued but not urgent tonight.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Content update. The launch newsletter is pre-scheduled and screenshot-reviewed per Joshua's directive — A-063 is effectively closed pending final CMS verification from Dave. Faith lessons 8 and 9 are drafted, A-071, and they're with Peterson for review. Lesson 10 on Community is outlined and ready to write once Peterson clears 8-9. I also have one podcast MDX file drafted for A-053 that needs a commit. Tonight I want to push faith content forward — if Peterson gives me notes on 8-9 in the next hour, I can have lesson 10 drafted and lessons 8-9 polished by end of session.

transcript.speaker: Peterson
transcript.message: I'm reviewing lessons 8 and 9 right now — A-089. Lesson 8 is solid, good dimensional coverage on stewardship. Lesson 9 needs a stronger scripture anchor; the current reference feels tangential rather than foundational. I'll have specific notes back to Alex within 30 minutes. Also confirming Jett's vaccine appointment happened today per A-086 — that's resolved. On the broader faith content, I want to make sure we're not rushing to hit 15-20 lessons at the expense of depth. Each lesson should leave someone with a genuine takeaway they can act on.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: Quick market context for the record. Fear and Greed at 38, SPY regime sideways, paper equity at $99,849 across 4 positions. Zero buy signals today. No action required — the $75K cash stays liquid per Joshua's standing directive. I'll note the market posture supports patience. Nothing here should distract from the launch push.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Quick cross-talk — anyone have a dependency or challenge? Elon (CTO): James, once I push the analytics hooks PR tonight, how fast can you validate? I want to know if we're shipping a clean PR to Joshua or if there'll be iteration.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: If you push by 9 PM, I'll have validation results by 9:30. The test harness is literally sitting there waiting. If any events fail, I'll flag the specific hook and you can patch in minutes — these are lightweight event calls, not complex logic.

transcript.speaker: Peterson
transcript.message: Alex, on lesson 9 — swap the Proverbs 3:5 reference for something from James chapter 1. It ties the perseverance theme much tighter to the dimension. I'll send you the exact verses in my written notes.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Got it. James 1:2-4 is perfect for that. I'll rework lesson 9's anchor and have it back to you for a final glance before I move to lesson 10.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good exchanges. Here's the evening session priority stack. Number one: Elon stages the analytics Swift hooks PR for Joshua's review — this is the last piece Joshua needs to merge for the day-one patch. Number two: James validates analytics events the moment hooks land — we want a clean PR, not a revision cycle. Number three: Alex pushes faith content forward — fix lesson 9 with Peterson's notes, draft lesson 10, commit the podcast MDX file. Supporting work: Dave does final CMS dry run, Elon burns down the 12 uncommitted hub files and the dead-code TODOs, Steve does visual review once the build compiles. One decision we need from Joshua: the analytics PR will be ready for his review tonight or first thing tomorrow — does he want to merge tonight or queue it for morning? That determines whether James can validate against a live build or just against the staged code. Let's execute.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message:
**Theo (COO) — Closing Remarks:**

Two clear decisions from Joshua. First, the analytics PR merges tonight — conditional on James's validation passing clean. That means Elon and James, your 9:00/9:30 handoff isn't optional, it's the critical path. If James flags any failures, Elon patches immediately and James re-validates. We do not send Joshua a dirty PR. If it passes clean, Joshua merges tonight and we ship a fully loaded day-one patch. Second, faith content launches at 10 lessons, not 15. Th

actionItems.text: Stage analytics Swift hooks PR with all 6 event types for Joshua's review-and-merge (close A-087)
Run analytics validation tests against staged hooks and report pass/fail within 30 min of PR push (advance A-088)
Review and merge analytics Swift hooks PR once validation passes (advance A-075)
Fix lesson 9 scripture anchor (swap to James 1:2-4), draft lesson 10 on Community dimension (advance A-071, A-082)
Return lesson 9 scripture notes to Alex within 30 minutes, final review of lesson 8 clearance (close A-089)
Commit 12 uncommitted hub files and remove 3 dead-code TODOs (advance A-090, close A-076)
Wire welcome modal + Compass empty state copy into day-one patch build and compile (close A-074)
Final CMS dry run to confirm one-click newsletter send works end-to-end (close A-081)
Commit drafted podcast MDX file to website repo (close A-053, advance A-092)
Visual review of empty state copy rendering in compiled build — verify hierarchy and spacing (advance A-050)
Finalize launch metrics dashboard spec with 150-target benchmark and day-3 escalation trigger (close A-054)
Continue monitoring Apple review status and alert team immediately on approval (A-093)

topPriorities: Elon stages analytics Swift hooks PR (A-087) for Joshua's review-and-merge
James validates all 6 analytics event types immediately after hooks land (A-088)
Alex fixes lesson 9 scripture anchor, drafts lesson 10, commits podcast MDX (A-071, A-082, A-053)

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Should faith content integrate into the Family tab? → Keep faith content as separate standalone section
Deploy idle cash ($75,340)? → No action — keep cash liquid
How deep should standalone faith content go? → Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
What is the minimum viable scope for the Lever App Store submission? → Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
Should we unify the dual Supabase instances (Hub vs Website) this sprint or defer? → Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now
Do we have a privacy policy and terms of service URL ready for the App Store submission? → Need to draft and host tonight — use a simple template
Should we define a paid tier structure before or after the App Store launch? → Launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on

Should we prioritize drafting the privacy policy and App Store listing assets (screenshots, description, keywords) THIS sprint as the critical path to submission, or defer to tomorrow? → Ship privacy policy template and draft listing assets tonight
Should we submit the App Store listing as soon as the draft is ready, or do you want a review pass before we hit submit? → Queue draft for Joshua review before submitting
Have you reviewed the App Store listing draft (A-015)? The build is ready to package and submit the moment you approve. → Approve listing as-is and greenlight submission

Once the App Store submission goes through and enters review, should we immediately begin instrumenting analytics (A-020) or prioritize expanding faith lessons to 15-20 (A-013)? → Analytics instrumentation first — we need data before expanding content
Should we set a target date for enabling the paid tier in the Lever app? → Defer decision until we see initial download data
Should we unify the Compass UI across website and mobile app before users encounter both, or keep them separate for now? → Defer until we see which surface gets more usage via analytics

When the app clears review, should we do a soft launch (no promotion, organic only) or immediately push it through newsletter and podcast? → Medium push — announce via newsletter and one podcast mention
When Apple approves the app, should the launch newsletter go out same-day or be scheduled for the following morning? → Same-day if approved before 2pm, otherwise next morning
What is your week-one download target for the Lever app to calibrate launch promotion intensity? → 150 downloads (modest organic + newsletter push)
Which newsletter subject line style do you prefer for the launch announcement? → Story-driven (e.g. 'I built an app. Here's why.')
Should the empty state patches (Family + Compass tabs) block the launch newsletter push, or ship as a same-day post-launch update? → Launch as-is, ship empty state patch as day-one update

Should we pre-schedule the launch newsletter in the CMS (ready to send with one click) or keep it as a manual send so you control exact timing? → Pre-schedule but require Joshua's Slack confirmation before auto-send

The launch newsletter is being pre-scheduled in the CMS tonight per your directive. Do you want to review it in the morning via Slack screenshot, or do a live CMS preview before arming the send? → Slack screenshot review (faster, async)

Once all overnight tasks close, should we shift sprint focus to content expansion (faith lessons 8-15) or technical debt (7 TODOs/FIXMEs, dual Supabase, content sync automation)? → Split — half the team on content, half on tech debt
Should we add a lightweight welcome modal (3 sentences, no tutorial) to the day-one post-launch patch? → Yes — add welcome modal to the day-one patch
How should Joshua allocate the 2-hour build window this morning? → Newsletter screenshot review first (2 min), then crash reporting check (5 min), then analytics scaffolding (remaining time)

If Apple approves the app today (Friday), do you want to launch-push today or hold for Monday morning for better newsletter open rates? → Same-day push but schedule a follow-up Monday reminder newsletter
Did you complete the three morning tasks: (1) newsletter screenshot review/approval, (2) crash reporting verification, (3) analytics scaffolding? → Newsletter + crash done, analytics not started

Elon and James want to stage the analytics Swift hooks PR so you only need to review and merge (5 min) instead of coding it yourself (~60 min). Approve this approach? → Yes — let them stage it, I'll review and merge
Should Alex draft the Monday follow-up reminder newsletter now or wait until we see initial download numbers after launch? → Draft a generic version now and personalize with data Sunday night

ceoDecisions.question: The analytics Swift hooks PR will be staged and validated tonight. Do you want to review-and-merge it tonight (enabling a fully loaded day-one patch) or queue it for your morning window?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Merge tonight but only if James's validation passes clean
options: Merge tonight if PR is ready by 10 PM
Merge tonight but only if James's validation passes clean

ceoDecisions.question: We now have 10 faith lessons drafted or in review (7 shipped + lessons 8, 9, 10 in pipeline). Should we push to hit 15 lessons before launch promotion, or is 10 sufficient for launch with the rest added post-launch?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Launch at 10, commit to shipping 15 within first week post-launch
options: Launch with whatever is ready when Apple approves (likely 10)
Hold newsletter push until we hit 15 lessons
Launch at 10, commit to shipping 15 within first week post-launch
